title: Monday Mobile App search function needs improving to match browser
excerpt:
The mobile Apps search function is different to Monday.com browser based. This means you must search the exact board title instead of partial searches. 
Use Case: We document building works and our boards are titled by p…

posts.author: BenWalker (Ben)
posts.cookedHtml:
The mobile Apps search function is different to Monday.com browser based. This means you must search the exact board title instead of partial searches.

Use Case: We document building works and our boards are titled by project address. If a project has risk flags we use the warning triangle symbol at the start of the title.

When search for a property on Monday.com browser you can use the numbers, street or postcode.

When searching on the mobile app you must type the exact address. This means we have to search the exact board address to find them.

Can this be changed to match the search input criteria of the web based search. Simple change but very effective to team that operate on tablets.

posts.author: sanmart (sanmart)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree with this. The mobile app search should work the same as the browser search.

On web, we can search using part of an address (street name, number, postcode), but on mobile we must type the exact board title. This makes it hard for teams using phones or tablets on-site.

Matching the mobile search to the web search would be a simple change with a big usability improvement.
